memberdef.argsstring: (float s00, float s01, float s02, float s10, float s11, float s12, float s20, float s21, float s22)
memberdef.name: Matrix3

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
s00
First element
s01
Second element
s02
Third element
s10
Fourth element
s11
Fifth element
s12
Sixth element
s20
Seventh element
s21
Eighth element
s22
Ninth element

@SINCE_1_9.21
matrix
A reference to the moved matrix
A reference to this

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied matrix

detaileddescription.para: Utilizes appropriate machine epsilon values.

@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
The Matrix to compare this to
True if the matrices are equal
Utilizes appropriate machine epsilon values.

@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
The Matrix to compare this to
true if the matrices are equal

briefdescription.para: Sets the matrix to the identity matrix. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Returns the contents of the matrix as an array of 9 floats.

detaileddescription.para: The order of the values for a matrix is:

@SINCE_1_0.0
The matrix contents as an array of 9 floats

briefdescription.para: Returns the contents of the matrix as an array of 9 floats.

detaileddescription.para: The order of the values for a matrix is:

@SINCE_1_0.0
The matrix contents as an array of 9 floats

@SINCE_1_0.0
True if successful

briefdescription.para: Multiplies all elements of the matrix by the scale value.
Magnitude returns the average of the absolute values of the elements * 3.

detaileddescription.para:
(The Magnitude of the unit matrix is therefore 1) @SINCE_1_0.0
The magnitude - always positive

briefdescription.para: If the matrix is invertible, then this method inverts, transposes and scales the matrix such that the resultant element values average 1.

detaileddescription.para: If the matrix is not invertible, then the matrix is left unchanged.

@SINCE_1_0.0
true if the matrix is invertible, otherwise false
Returned Matrix = This Matrix * rhs

It makes some memory allocate & copy internally. Use Matrix3::Multiply API for time critical path.
@SINCE_2_1.44
rhs
The Matrix to multiply this by
A Matrix containing the result
This Matrix *= rhs

It makes some memory allocate & copy internally.
@SINCE_2_1.46
rhs
The Matrix to multiply this by
Itself
Returned Vector = This Matrix * rhs

@SINCE_2_1.34
rhs
The Vector3 to multiply this by
A Vector3 containing the result

briefdescription.para: Function to multiply two matrices and store the result onto third.

detaileddescription.para: Use this method in time critical path as it does not require temporaries
result = rhs * lhs

@SINCE_1_0.0
result
Result of the multiplication
lhs
Matrix, this can be same matrix as result
rhs
Matrix, this cannot be same matrix as result
The matrix is stored as a flat array and is Column Major, i.e. the storage order is as follows (numbers represent indices of array):
Each axis is contiguous in memory, so the x-axis corresponds to elements 0, 1 and 2, the y-axis corresponds to elements 3, 4 and 5, and the z-axis corresponds to elements 6, 7 and 8.
@SINCE_1_0.0
